1: Great creative force is present—and so is the risk of forcing outcomes, burning out, or acting before the time is ripe.
2: Receptive strength means carrying what is real; it can also mean passivity, being led astray, or carrying too much for others.
3: Beginnings are tangled and unclear. “Success” here assumes patience and sorting mess—rash starts make things worse.
4: Immaturity and blind spots; growth needs teaching, not cleverness. “Success” is conditional on humility.
5: Waiting is the lesson—impatience and premature action are the usual mistakes, not lack of talent.
6: Opposition and blockage: even “good fortune” here is about knowing when to stop fighting, not winning every point.
7: Discipline and collective force—also the danger of conformity, harsh leadership, or marching without a humane aim.
8: Alliance and loyalty—also the risk of cliques, dependency, and unexamined “togetherness.”
9: Small accumulations and restraint; progress is partial. Full breakthrough is still withheld—don’t over-read a sunny word.
10: Walking near danger: tact buys safety, not immunity. Complacency after a narrow escape is a classic slip.
11: Harmony and flow—still subject to reversal; ease can breed neglect when conditions turn.
12: Closure and mismatch: the superior person retreats to inner truth; outer advance is not favored.
13: Fellowship in openness—also faction, performance of unity, and mistaking crowd for clarity.
14: Great holdings—envy, burden, and the moral cost of power attend real abundance.
15: Modesty steadies; false humility and self-erasure are the shadow sides.
16: Enthusiasm can mobilize—or manipulate, scatter, or crash after the high wears off.
17: Following the time—also following blindly, mood-chasing, or losing your own axis.
18: Rot in the pattern: repair is possible but costly; family/system wounds don’t heal by optimism alone.
19: Approach and growth—Wilhelm adds a coming downturn (“eighth month”); don’t assume the curve stays up.
20: Looking and being seen: exposure cuts both ways; image without substance fails.
21: Justice and bite-through: clarity can become cruelty; penalties need proportion.
22: Grace and surface polish—distraction by appearance, vanity, and style over depth.
23: Stripping away: erosion of support; the text warns against pushing forward as if things were normal.
24: Return of light—small, fragile; don’t confuse first stirrings with full recovery.
25: Natural correctness—also accident, recklessness disguised as innocence, and disproportionate fallout.
26: Gathering strength and learning—also hoarding, dogma, and delaying right action.
27: Nourishment: what you feed (body, speech, attention) builds or poisons; neglect and greed both misalign.
28: Overload: structure near breaking; bravado and “push through” thinking worsen the sag.
29: Danger repeated: sincerity helps you endure, not erase hazard. Risk and anxiety are structural here.
30: Clarity and attachment to what gives light—also burnout, dazzle, and clinging past usefulness.
31: Influence and attraction—haste in bonding, seduction without commitment, mistaking chemistry for fit.
32: Duration—also rigidity, habit for its own sake, and mistaking motion for meaning.
33: Retreat as strategy, not cowardice; pursuing more when heaven says yield deepens loss.
34: Great strength—danger is using force where measure and law are required.
35: Advance and visibility—envy, haste, and hollow recognition travel with the rise.
36: Light wounded or hidden: injury, slander, or necessary concealment; perseverance is sober, not cheerful.
37: The household: roles, loyalties, and private order—dysfunction here is intimate and consequential.
38: Opposition and distance: trust is local and small; large unity is not promised.
39: Obstruction and limping: progress is slow, directional (not all paths help), and may need help you don’t yet have.
40: Release after tension—old patterns can snap back; forgiveness isn’t the same as forgetting risk.
41: Decrease and austerity: loss and restraint bite; “success” is tied to sincerity under reduction, not gain.
42: Increase—also inflation of ego, overextension, and mistaking more for better.
43: Breakthrough and open declaration—danger, backlash, and the cost of forcing truth into the open.
44: Sudden encounter with what is seductive or subversive; binding where you shouldn’t is the recurring harm.
45: Gathering—also panic herds, armed anxiety, and mistaking assembly for right purpose.
46: Rising by degrees—still dependent on help, season, and not skipping rungs; fearlessness isn’t recklessness.
47: Oppression: depletion, doubt, words not believed; “success” here is endurance and integrity under strain, not ease.
48: The well: common resource—neglect, freeloading, and poisoned sources are the failures of the pattern.
49: Revolution: real change needs timing and trust; forced or cosmetic change backfires.
50: The cauldron: transformation of substance—also pressure to “be useful,” ritual empty of heart.
51: Shock: alarm that reorders life—trauma, instability, and the temptation to perform calm instead of change.
52: Stillness—also numbness, avoidance, and freezing when movement is due.
53: Gradualness—impatience and skipped steps wreck this pattern; marriage imagery is serious, not decorative.
54: The marrying maiden: misalignment, second place, and undertakings that don’t truly further.
55: Peak fullness—also hubris at noon, lawsuits, and the turn toward decline built into the height.
56: The wanderer: marginality, small margins, and the loneliness of not being home.
57: Gentle penetration—also nagging, invisible pressure, and influence without accountability.
58: Joy and exchange—also gossip, addiction to pleasure, and agreement that avoids hard truth.
59: Dispersion—dissolving blocks can also mean scattering focus, faith, or responsibility.
60: Limit and measure—too loose fails; too tight becomes galling and unsustainable.
61: Inner truth—also naivete, misplaced trust, and “sincerity” shown to the wrong objects.
62: Small excess: stay low; overreaching in small matters is the specific warning.
63: After completion: order already slipping; vigilance at the end matters more than celebration.
64: Almost across—almost invites careless hurry; the fox’s tail in the water is unfinished business made worse.
